nodes.note: Fires on any incoming message. In the live bot the user sends a VOICE NOTE; the raw update carries message.voice.file_id, which the next nodes read as an expression (Telegram normalize treats voice as a generic message kind here, so the file_id rides through on $json).
STT leg (PB-T7): the host downloads the voice file via ctx.tg.getFile, then transcribes it via ctx.ai.transcribe. The text lands at $json.transcript.text.
ai:model PROVIDER (PB-T5): supplies which model + credential the agent calls (the OpenRouter-style model id). Resolved as config, never run as a step; attaches to the agent's ai:model slot.

ai:memory PROVIDER (PB-T4): a rolling Postgres-backed conversation memory keyed by chat id. The agent replays prior turns before answering and persists the new turn after; attaches to the agent's ai:memory slot.

params.description: Use this scratchpad to reason step-by-step before answering the caller.

nodes.note: ai:tool PROVIDER (PB-T6): the Think tool — a no-op reasoning scratchpad that improves multi-step answers; attaches to the agent's repeatable ai:tool slot.

params.description: Look up the live status of a customer order by its id.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The order id to look up

nodes.note: ai:tool PROVIDER (PB-T6): an HTTP tool the model can call to fetch a live order status. The model produces {orderId}, the host runs ctx.http.request and feeds the body back.

params.system_prompt: You are a friendly Persian voice-support agent. Answer the caller concisely. Use the order_status tool when they ask about an order, and the think tool to plan multi-step answers.

nodes.note: The orchestrator (PB-T5): consumes the model/memory/tool providers via its typed slots and turns the transcribed question into a reply at $json.ai.reply.
TTS leg (PB-T7): synthesizes the agent's reply to audio via ctx.ai.speech, stores the bytes via ctx.files.write, and surfaces a CTB file id at $json.speech.fileId (opus suits a Telegram voice note).
Sends the synthesized audio back to the caller (source:file → the host reads the stored bytes and uploads them). This closes the voice loop, rebuilding the user's screenshot end to end.
